issues: The headline 'Ex-Clipper Hardy Ready for Game Three' does not appear in the OCR — no clear headline is present in the extracted text; the OCR begins directly with the byline/staff writer attribution.
The byline is listed as 'Staff Writer' in the OCR but the draft labels it 'Staff Writer' as a named byline rather than a role — however this is acceptable as-is; no named reporter is given.

The long description omits the notable detail that Hardy had 39 goals and 72 assists last season with Nanaimo (his career-best) and that his point totals dropped significantly with Richmond (19G, 33A), which is central to the article's framing.

The long description does not mention Hardy's linemates (Brian Kozak and Jason Phillips) or the other top Richmond scorers named (Dave Tomlinson, Rob Clarke, Bryan Moller), which are relevant Sockeyes details from the OCR.

The OCR also contains substantial adjacent-column noise about an unrelated Don Rintala baseball/New Zealand story and a curling/TSN segment — the draft correctly focuses on the Hardy/Sockeyes article, but the Don Rintala blurb briefly mentions him as 'A former captain with Richmond Sockeyes,' which is an additional Sockeyes reference the draft ignores.
Game three is described as being at Civic Arena at 8 p.m. with over 1,300 expected — this context is in the draft but could be more precise.
review.correctedDescriptionShort: Bill Hardy, a former Nanaimo Clipper now with the Richmond Sockeyes, is profiled ahead of game three of the BCJHL Coastal Division semi-final series at Civic Arena, with Richmond holding a 2-0 series lead.
review.correctedDescriptionLong: Richmond Sockeyes hold a 2-0 lead over the Nanaimo Clippers heading into game three of the BCJHL Coastal Division best-of-seven semi-final, scheduled for 8 p.m. at Civic Arena with over 1,300 fans expected. The article profiles Bill Hardy, who spent four years with Nanaimo — posting 39 goals and 72 assists in his best season — before joining Richmond in hopes of winning a championship and earning a college scholarship. With the Sockeyes, Hardy's role shifted to a grind-and-corner game behind top scorers Dave Tomlinson, Rob Clarke, and Bryan Moller, with linemates Brian Kozak and Jason Phillips; coach Orland Kurtenbach is quoted praising Hardy's contributions. A separate adjacent-column item also briefly identifies Don Rintala as a former Richmond Sockeyes captain, now playing fastball in New Zealand.
review.notes: No clear standalone headline is present in the OCR for the Hardy article — the text begins directly with "Staff Writer." The AI-invented headline should be removed or left blank. Also note the adjacent column contains a secondary Sockeyes reference: Don Rintala is identified as a former Sockeyes captain — editors may wish to log this as a separate scan or add a note.
